Items.Title: 加密货币困境：加密货币如何迷失方向😞 | The Crypto Dilemma: How crypto lost its way 😞
Items.Description:
这一切都始于权力下放的想法。 不受政府控制和操纵的去中心化货币的想法是革命性的。 它承诺公平竞争并将权力还给人民💪。 但在这个过程中的某个地方，事情变得扭曲了。 加密货币从一种革命性的方式从大人物手中夺回权力，变成了另一种让富人变得更富有的方式。

交易和不确定性📈📉

使用加密货币购买一杯咖啡或支付租金的日子已经一去不复返了。 现在一切都是关于 HODLing 🔥（对于所有常态来说，这就是“持有”）并看着价值上涨，上涨，上涨。 但说实话，加密货币的价值就像暴风雨中的纸牌屋一样稳定。 前一分钟你是百万富翁，下一分钟你又变回了普通的 Jeffery 🙍‍♂️。 加密货币价格的波动性使其很难用作日常交易的稳定货币。 这就像试图在加密跷跷板上保持平衡，前一分钟你在上升，下一分钟你就下降了。

甚至不要让我开始 ICO（首次代币发行）。 这就像狂野的西部，任何拥有白皮书和梦想的人都可以推出他们自己的“革命性”加密货币。 这就像一场永无止境的加密鸡游戏🐔，每个人都试图通过更大更好的承诺来超越对方。 这就像一场加密货币嘉年华，每个人都试图向您推销下一件大事，但其中大多数只是空的加密货币外壳。

但老实说，这些项目中的大多数都只是雾里看花。 它们没有真正的用途，没有真正的目的。 这就像买彩票🎟️，但你购买的不是百万美元的机会，而是几个月后可能一文不值的数字代币的机会。 这就像玩加密轮盘赌，你可能会中奖，也可能一无所有，只剩下一堆没有实际价值的数字代币。

解决方案？

那么解决方案是什么？ 我们如何让加密货币重回正轨并兑现其最初的承诺？

好吧，首先，我们需要停止将其视为快速致富的计划，并开始将其用于其预期目的：作为一种交换手段。 我们需要开始使用它来买东西、支付账单、进行实际交易。 这就是我们创造真正价值和效用的方式。 这就像加密训练营，我们需要回到基础并开始将其用于预期目的。

我们需要开始对我们投资的项目更有选择性。仅仅因为它是“加密货币”并不意味着它是一项好的投资。 在交出我们辛苦赚来的现金之前，我们需要进行尽职调查并实际调查团队、技术和用例。 这就像加密约会，你需要确保你投资的是正确的。

加密货币的未来🔮

那么，加密货币的未来是什么样的？ 好吧，很难肯定地说，但有一件事是肯定的：我们将加密货币用于其预期目的的次数越多，它的价值和实用性就越大。 随着越来越多的企业和个人开始看到使用加密货币的好处，它将变得越来越主流。 这就像加密进化，它仍处于早期阶段，但随着越来越多的人开始看到它的好处，它会继续成长和进化🌎。

但我们需要现实一点，承认加密货币永远无法完全取代传统形式的货币。 它永远是一个利基市场，总会有一定程度的波动。 然而，这并不意味着加密货币不能对金融和银行业产生真正的影响。 它就像加密香料，它可能不是主要成分，但它可以为传统金融系统增加一个全新的维度。

加密有可能使银行服务的获取民主化，特别是在传统银行系统不发达的发展中国家。 它还可以提供传统银行系统无法比拟的安全和隐私级别。 区块链技术是大多数加密货币的基础，可用于各种行业以提高安全性和透明度。 它就像加密货币瑞士军刀，有很多用途和潜力。

外卖💭

所以，让我们放下 Kool-Aid，开始对加密更加挑剔。 是时候追根溯源了，记住我们最初爱上它的原因。 因为归根结底，加密货币并不是为了快速赚钱，而是为了重新控制我们的财务未来。 是时候停止成为加密沙发土豆并开始将其用于预期目的了。

我们可以一起让加密再次伟大！ 最初发布在我的博客上



原文:

It all started with the idea of decentralization. The idea of a decentralized currency, free from government control and manipulation, was revolutionary. It promised to level the playing field and give power back to the people 💪. But somewhere along the way, things got twisted. Crypto went from being a revolutionary way to take power back from the big guys to just another way for the rich to get richer.

Trading and Uncertainty 📈📉

Gone are the days of using crypto to buy a cup of coffee or pay your rent. Now it's all about HODLing 🔥 (that's "hold" for all you normies out there) and watching the value go up, up, up. But let's be real, the value of crypto is about as stable as a house of cards in a storm. One minute you're a millionaire, the next you're back to being a regular Jeffery 🙍‍♂️. The volatile nature of crypto prices makes it difficult for it to be used as a stable currency for day-to-day transactions. It's like trying to balance on a crypto-see-saw, one minute you're up, the next you're down.

The Wild West of ICOs 🏇🏽

And don't even get me started on the ICOs (initial coin offerings). It's like the Wild West out there and anyone with a white paper and a dream can launch their own "revolutionary" cryptocurrency. It's like a never-ending game of crypto-chicken 🐔, with everyone trying to outdo each other with bigger and better promises. The lack of regulation and oversight in the ICO market has led to a proliferation of scams and worthless projects, further damaging the reputation of crypto as a legitimate form of currency. It's like a crypto-carnival and everyone's trying to sell you the next big thing, but most of them are just empty crypto-shells.

But let's be honest, most of these projects are just smoke and mirrors. They have no real utility, no real purpose. It's like buying a lottery ticket 🎟️, but instead of a chance at a million dollars, you're buying a chance at a digital token that may or may not be worth anything in a few months. It's like playing crypto roulette, you might hit the jackpot or you might be left with nothing but a bunch of digital token with no real value.

The Solution?

So what's the solution? How do we get crypto back on track and living up to its original promise?

Well, first things first, we need to stop treating it like a get-rich-quick scheme and start using it for what it was intended for: as a means of exchange. We need to start using it to buy things, to pay bills, to do actual transactions. That's how we'll create real value and utility. It's like crypto-bootcamp, we need to get back to the basics and start using it for its intended purpose.

And we need to start being more selective about the projects we invest in. Just because it's a "crypto" doesn't mean it's a good investment. We need to do our due diligence and actually look into the team, the technology, and the use case before handing over our hard-earned cash. It's like crypto-dating, you need to make sure you're investing in the right one.

The Future of Crypto 🔮

So, what does the future of crypto look like? Well, it's hard to say for sure, but one thing is certain: the more we use crypto for its intended purpose, the more value and utility it will have. And as more and more businesses and individuals start to see the benefits of using crypto, the more mainstream it will become. It's like crypto-evolution, it's still in its early stages but as more people start to see the benefits, it will continue to grow and evolve 🌎.

But we need to be realistic and acknowledge that crypto will never be able to fully replace traditional forms of currency. It will always be a niche market and there will always be a certain level of volatility. However, that doesn't mean that crypto can't have a real impact on the world of finance and banking. It's like crypto-spice, it may not be the main ingredient but it can add a whole new dimension to traditional financial systems.

Crypto has the potential to democratize access to banking services, particularly in developing countries where traditional banking systems are not as developed. It can also provide a level of security and privacy that traditional banking systems can't match. Blockchain technology, which underlies most crypto, can be used in a variety of industries to improve security and transparency. It's like crypto-Swiss Army knife, it has many uses and potentials.

The Takeaway 💭

So, let's put down the Kool-Aid and start being more critical about crypto. It's time to get back to the roots and remember why we fell in love with it in the first place. Because at the end of the day, crypto isn't about making a quick buck, it's about taking back control of our financial future. It's time to stop being crypto-couch potatoes and start using it for its intended purpose.

Together, we can make crypto great again! 🚀

Items.Title: Python 中的线性回归：从数据到模型 | Linear Regression in Python: From Data to Model
Items.Description:
线性回归是一种统计方法，用于对因变量（也称为结果或响应变量）与一个或多个自变量（也称为预测变量或解释变量）之间的关系进行建模。 线性回归的目标是通过一组数据点找到最佳拟合线，其中线由形式为 y = mx + b 的方程定义，其中 y 是因变量，x 是自变量， m 是直线的斜率，b 是 y 截距。 线性回归可用于简单线性回归（一个自变量）和多元线性回归（多个自变量）。

导入库

import pandas as pd import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt %matplotlib inline

将训练和测试数据集加载到 pandas 数据框中

train_df = pd.read_csv("/kaggle/input/random-linear-regression/train.csv") #Drop null values train_df = train_df.dropna() train_df.head()
是
是
自变量和因变量的选择

我们在您的数据框中选择了要用于 x 和 y 轴的列。 例如，如果您有一个名为“x”的列代表自变量，一个名为“y”的列代表因变量，您可以像这样选择这些列：

 train_x = train_df['x'] train_y = train_df['y'] test_x = test_df['x'] test_y = test_df['y']

可视化训练数据

要使用数据框绘制线性图，我们使用 Python 中流行的数据可视化库 Matplotlib。 我们在上面导入了它。

现在我们使用plt.scatter()函数绘制数据点，并使用plt.plot()函数绘制最佳拟合线。

我们还使用numpy.polyfit()函数将一条线拟合到数据点，并获得最佳拟合线的斜率和 y 轴截距。

 coefficients = np.polyfit(train_x, train_y, 1) m, b = coefficients plt.scatter(train_x, train_y) plt.plot(train_x, m*train_x + b) plt.xlabel('train_x') plt.ylabel('train_y') plt.show()

可视化测试数据

coefficients = np.polyfit(test_x, test_y, 1) m, b = coefficients plt.scatter(test_x, test_y) plt.plot(test_x, m*test_x + b) plt.xlabel('test_x') plt.ylabel('test_y') plt.show()

模型创建、训练和测试

要创建线性回归模型并使用您的数据框训练和测试数据，我们可以使用 Python 中的scikit-learn库。 第一步是导入库和您要使用的特定模型。

例如，我们使用LinearRegression模块中的sklearn.linear_model类：

 from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

创建模型的实例。

 model = LinearRegression()

现在，我们使用fit()方法在训练数据上训练模型：

 train_x = train_x.values.reshape(-1, 1) test_x = test_x.values.reshape(-1, 1)
 model.fit(train_x, train_y)

使用以下命令检查模型的系数和截距：

 print("Coefficients: ",model.coef_) print("Intercept: ",model.intercept_)
 Coefficients: [1.00065638] Intercept: -0.10726546430097272

我们的模型已经训练好了，现在我们可以使用predict()方法对测试数据进行预测：

 y_pred = model.predict(test_x)

评估模型性能

我们可以通过比较预测值和实际值来评估模型的性能。 有许多评估指标，例如mean_absolute_error 、 mean_squared_error或r2_score 。

 from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score print("Mean Absolute Error: ",mean_absolute_error(test_y, y_pred)) print("Mean Squared Error: ",mean_squared_error(test_y, y_pred)) print("R2 Score: ",r2_score(test_y, y_pred))
 Mean Absolute Error: 2.415771850041258 Mean Squared Error: 9.432922192039305 R2 Score: 0.9888014444327563

可视化模型性能

我们还可以通过使用与之前相同的方法绘制测试数据点和预测线来可视化结果。

 plt.scatter(test_x, test_y) plt.plot(test_x, y_pred, color='r') plt.xlabel('x') plt.ylabel('y') plt.show()

结尾！ 希望你喜欢这个...

原文:

Linear regression is a statistical method used for modeling the relationship between a dependent variable (also known as the outcome or response variable) and one or more independent variables (also known as predictors or explanatory variables). The goal of linear regression is to find the best-fitting line through a set of data points, where the line is defined by an equation of the form y = mx + b, where y is the dependent variable, x is the independent variable, m is the slope of the line, and b is the y-intercept. Linear regression can be used for both simple linear regression (one independent variable) and multiple linear regression (more than one independent variable).

Importing Libraries 

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline

Loding train and test dataset into pandas data frame 

train_df = pd.read_csv("/kaggle/input/random-linear-regression/train.csv")
#Drop null values
train_df = train_df.dropna() train_df.head()

3 38.0 36.586398
4 87.0 87.288984 
test_df = pd.read_csv("/kaggle/input/random-linear-regression/test.csv")
# Drop null values
test_df = test_df.dropna()
test_df.head()
x y
0 77 79.775152
1 21 23.177279
2 22 25.609262
3 20 17.857388
4 36 41.849864

Selection of independent and and dependent variable

We selected the columns in your data frame that we want to use for the x and y axis. For example, if you have a column called 'x' that represents the independent variable and a column called 'y' that represents the dependent variable, you can select those columns like this:

train_x = train_df['x']
train_y = train_df['y'] test_x = test_df['x']
test_y = test_df['y']

Visualizing the training data

To draw a linear graph using your data frame, we use the popular data visualization library in Python called Matplotlib. We imported it above.

Now we use the plt.scatter() function to plot the data points, and the plt.plot() function to plot the line of best fit.

We also use the numpy.polyfit() function to fit a line to the data points and get the slope and y-intercept of the line of best fit.

coefficients = np.polyfit(train_x, train_y, 1)
m, b = coefficients
plt.scatter(train_x, train_y)
plt.plot(train_x, m*train_x + b)
plt.xlabel('train_x')
plt.ylabel('train_y')
plt.show()

Visualizing test data 

coefficients = np.polyfit(test_x, test_y, 1)
m, b = coefficients
plt.scatter(test_x, test_y)
plt.plot(test_x, m*test_x + b)
plt.xlabel('test_x')
plt.ylabel('test_y')
plt.show()

Model Creation, training, and testing

To create a linear regression model and train and test the data using your data frame, we can use the scikit-learn library in Python. The first step is to import the library and the specific model you want to use.

For example, we use the LinearRegression class from the sklearn.linear_model module:

from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

Create an instance of the model.

model = LinearRegression()

Now, we use the fit() method to train the model on the training data:

train_x = train_x.values.reshape(-1, 1)
test_x = test_x.values.reshape(-1, 1)

model.fit(train_x, train_y)

Check the coefficients of the model and the intercept using following command:

print("Coefficients: ",model.coef_)
print("Intercept: ",model.intercept_)

Coefficients:  [1.00065638]
Intercept:  -0.10726546430097272

Our model is trained, now we can use the predict() method to make predictions on the test data:

y_pred = model.predict(test_x)

Evaluating model performance

We can evaluate the performance of the model by comparing the predicted values with the actual values. There are many evaluation metrics such as mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error or r2_score.

from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score print("Mean Absolute Error: ",mean_absolute_error(test_y, y_pred))
print("Mean Squared Error: ",mean_squared_error(test_y, y_pred))
print("R2 Score: ",r2_score(test_y, y_pred))

Mean Absolute Error:  2.415771850041258
Mean Squared Error:  9.432922192039305
R2 Score:  0.9888014444327563

Visualizing model performance

We can also visualize the results by plotting the test data points and the predicted line using the same approach as before.

plt.scatter(test_x, test_y)
plt.plot(test_x, y_pred, color='r')
plt.xlabel('x')
plt.ylabel('y')
plt.show()

Items.Description:
相反，我们使用分层数据模板，它允许我们对两个树节点进行模板化，同时控制将哪个属性用作节点子对象的源。 树视图标记表示 XAML 中的 WPF TreeView 控件。

树视图标记表示 XAML 中的 WPF TreeView 控件。

树视图表示父节点可以展开或折叠的父子关系的层次视图中的数据。 创建树视图

带标题的项目控件

如果使用可扩展应用程序标记语言 (XAML) 定义树视图，则可以指定 TreeViewItem 控件的标头内容以及从其集合创建的项。 上图说明了此方法。

您还可以将项目源指定为数据源，然后指定一个 HeaderTemplate 和 ItemTemplate 来定义 TreeViewItem 内容。

您还可以使用 HierarchicalDataTemplate 对象来定义 TreeViewItem 控件的布局。 有关详细信息和示例，请使用 SelectedValue、SelectedValuePath 和 SelectedItem。

硬编码的 WPF 树视图

首先，让我们看一下典型的两层 WPF 树视图，它严格使用以下代码片段中显示的值进行编码。

 <treeview name="myTreeView"> <treeviewitem header="Senior Employee" isselected="True"> <treeviewitem header="Kim Johnson"> <treeviewitem header="Total Employee"> <treeviewitem header="100"> </treeviewitem> <treeviewitem header="Working Days"> <treeviewitem header="Monday"> <treeviewitem header="Tuesday"> <treeviewitem header="Thursday"> </treeviewitem> </treeviewitem> <treeviewitem header="Junior Employee"> <treeviewitem header="David Paul"> <treeviewitem header="Total Employee"> <treeviewitem header="30"> </treeviewitem> <treeviewitem header="Work Days"> <treeviewitem header="Tuesday"> <treeviewitem header="Wednesday"> <treeviewitem header="Friday"> </treeviewitem> </treeviewitem> </treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeview>

输出：


在此示例中，我们看到了如何借助 XAML 文件创建普通的 TreeView，在输出中，我们看到我们创建了两个标头并向其中添加了 TreeItem。 我们在静态方法 XAML 文件中完成了所有这些工作。

动态树视图

现在我们已经学会了动态创建一个TreeView。

创建 WPF 树视图控件并在 XAML 中构造它的模板有很多，但在实际使用中，我们更可能从数据动态创建树视图控件。 它必须使用 C# 之类的代码，而不是 XAML。

阅读更多： Wpf 中的回退和目标空值是什么？

让我们从 XAML 开始：

 <treeview margin="0,0,0,0" x:name="DynamicTree"> </treeview>

评论项目对象也有一个“项目”成员，它是一个项目集合。 它通常形成一个层次结构。

我们需要向 TreeView 类的对象添加至少一个 TreeViewItem。 这将是 TreeView 的根节点。 通常，根只有一个节点，但也可能有多个根节点。 然后我们添加 TreeViewItem 添加对象到 TreeViewItem 对象中的相应项目。 下面的代码将创建一个简单的树：

 TreeViewItem HeaderPart = new TreeViewItem(); HeaderPart.Header = "Vehical"; DynamicTree.Items.Add(HeaderPart); // TreeViewItem Treeitems = new TreeViewItem(); Treeitems.Header = "Car"; HeaderPart.Items.Add(Treeitems); // TreeViewItem Treeitemsone = new TreeViewItem(); Treeitemsone.Header = "Bike"; HeaderPart.Items.Add(Treeitemsone); TreeViewItem SubTreeitems = new TreeViewItem(); SubTreeitems.Header = "Shine"; Treeitemsone.Items.Add(SubTreeitems); TreeViewItem SubTreeitemsone = new TreeViewItem(); SubTreeitemsone.Header = "Activa"; Treeitemsone.Items.Add(SubTreeitemsone); TreeViewItem SubTreeitemsTwo = new TreeViewItem(); SubTreeitemsTwo.Header = "FZ-5"; Treeitemsone.Items.Add(SubTreeitemsTwo); // TreeViewItem HeaderPartOne = new TreeViewItem(); HeaderPartOne.Header = "Air vehical"; DynamicTree.Items.Add(HeaderPartOne);

一个基本的数据绑定 TreeView

下面是前面代码创建的树：


截图 3：DataBinding TreeView

在 XAML 标记中，我们提到了 TreeView 项模板的分层数据模板。 我们通过设置模板的 Itemsource 属性来指示他使用 Items 属性来查找孩子的项目，并且在其中，我们定义了实际的模板，该模板现在仅包含一个绑定到 title 属性的文本块。

第一个示例非常简单，实际上简单到我们只是手动添加树视图对象，而不是生成一堆对象然后绑定它们。 然而，随着事情变得有点复杂，使用数据绑定的好处立即变得更加明显。

如前所述，两个模板已作为 TreeView 资源的一部分显示，允许 TreeView 根据要显示的数据类型选择适当的模板。 为 MyFamily Member 类型定义的模板是常规数据模板，因为此类型中没有子成员。 但是，如果我们希望每个家庭成员都有他们的孩子的集合，可能还有他们的孩子，我们可以使用分层模板。

在这两个示例中，我们都使用了代表家庭或家庭成员的图像，然后我们展示了一些有趣的数据，例如家庭成员的数量或人的年龄。

在 Code-Bank 中，我们简单地创建两个家族模式，用一组成员填充它们中的每一个，然后将每个家族添加到列表中，然后将其用作树视图的项目源。

结论

虽然完全可以像我们在上面的示例中所做的那样使用标记来定义完整的树视图，但这在大多数情况下并不是最好的方法，并且当您稍后对其进行编码时，这会导致更多的代码行. 再一次，解决方案是数据绑定。 使用数据绑定，树视图是高度可定制的，并且能够指定多个模板来表示不同的数据类型，可能性几乎是无限的。

The WPF tree view supports data binding, as all other WPF controls do, but, as the tree view hierarchy does, a normal DataTemplate is often not enough. Instead, we use the Hierarchical Data template, which allows us to template both tree nodes while controlling which property to use as a source for the node's child objects. The tree view tag represents the WPF TreeView control in XAML.

The tree view tag represents the WPF TreeView control in XAML.

A tree view represents data in a hierarchical view of the parent-child relationship where the parent node may expand or collapse. The left sidebar of Windows Explorer is an example of a tree view.

Creating a TreeView

TreeView control contains a hierarchy of TreeViewItem controls. A TreeViewItem control is a HeaderedItemsControl that contains a collection of headers and items. HeaderedItemsControl

If you are defining a tree view using the Extensible Application Markup Language (XAML), you can specify the header content of a TreeViewItem control and the items created from its collection. The previous illustration illustrates this method.

You can also specify the item source as the data source and then specify a HeaderTemplate and ItemTemplate to define the TreeViewItem content.

You can also use HierarchicalDataTemplate objects to define the layout of a TreeViewItem control. For more information and example use SelectedValue, SelectedValuePath, and SelectedItem.

A Hard-Coded WPF Tree View

To get started, let's look at a typical two-tier WPF tree view that is strictly coded with the values shown in the code snippet below.


    <treeview name="myTreeView">
        <treeviewitem header="Senior Employee" isselected="True">
            <treeviewitem header="Kim Johnson">
            <treeviewitem header="Total Employee">
                <treeviewitem header="100">
            </treeviewitem>
            <treeviewitem header="Working Days">
                <treeviewitem header="Monday">
                <treeviewitem header="Tuesday">
                <treeviewitem header="Thursday">
            </treeviewitem>
        </treeviewitem>
        <treeviewitem header="Junior Employee">
            <treeviewitem header="David Paul">
            <treeviewitem header="Total Employee">
                <treeviewitem header="30">
            </treeviewitem>
            <treeviewitem header="Work Days">
                <treeviewitem header="Tuesday">
                <treeviewitem header="Wednesday">
                <treeviewitem header="Friday">
            </treeviewitem>
        </treeviewitem>
    </treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeviewitem></treeview>

Output:


Screenshot_1: TreeView Output

In this example, we saw how to create a normal TreeView with the help of the XAML file and in the output, we saw that we created two headers and added TreeItem to it. We did all this in the XAML file which is a static method.

Dynamic TreeView

Now we have learned to dynamically create a TreeView.

There are many templates for creating WPF tree view control and constructing it in XAML but in actual use, we are more likely to dynamically create tree view control from data. It must be in code like C#, not XAML.

Read More: What Are Fallback And Target Null Values In Wpf?

Let's start with XAML:


    <treeview margin="0,0,0,0" x:name="DynamicTree">
</treeview>

There is a member in the Tree View class, "Items", which is an item collection. Item Collection for TreeView is a collection of Treeitems. Review item objects also have an "Items" member which is an item collection. It often forms a hierarchical structure.

We need to add at least one TreeViewItem to the object of the TreeView class. That will be the root node(s) of the TreeView. Typically, the root has a single node but multiple root nodes are possible. We then add the TreeViewItem add objects to the corresponding items in the TreeViewItem objects. The following code will make a simple tree:

  TreeViewItem HeaderPart = new TreeViewItem();
            HeaderPart.Header = "Vehical";
            DynamicTree.Items.Add(HeaderPart);
            //  
            TreeViewItem Treeitems = new TreeViewItem();
            Treeitems.Header = "Car";
            HeaderPart.Items.Add(Treeitems);
            //  
            TreeViewItem Treeitemsone = new TreeViewItem();
            Treeitemsone.Header = "Bike";
            HeaderPart.Items.Add(Treeitemsone);

            TreeViewItem SubTreeitems = new TreeViewItem();
            SubTreeitems.Header = "Shine";
            Treeitemsone.Items.Add(SubTreeitems);

            TreeViewItem SubTreeitemsone = new TreeViewItem();
            SubTreeitemsone.Header = "Activa";
            Treeitemsone.Items.Add(SubTreeitemsone);

            SubTreeitemsTwo.Header = "FZ-5";

The following is a tree created by the previous code:


Screenshot_2: Dynamic TreeView

Data binding and multiple templates

WPF supports TreeView data binding, as do all other WPF controls, but, as TreeView hierarchy does a normal Datatemplate is often not enough. Instead, we use the Hierarchical Data template, which allows us to template both tree nodes while controlling which property to use as a source for the node's child objects.

A basic data-bound TreeView

In the following example, We will show you how easy it is to get started with a hierarchical data template:

.XAML file>

  <grid margin="10">
        <treeview name="treehirmenu">
            <treeview.itemtemplate>
                <hierarchicaldatatemplate datatype="{x:Type MenuItem}" itemssource="{Binding Items}">
                    <textblock text="{Binding Title}">
                </textblock></hierarchicaldatatemplate>
            </treeview.itemtemplate>
        </treeview>
    </grid>

.CS File


  public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
            MenuItem Head = new MenuItem() { Title = "Tutorial" };
            MenuItem subdItem1 = new MenuItem() { Title = "Mobile Application" };
            subdItem1.Items.Add(new MenuItem() { Title = "Flutter" });
            subdItem1.Items.Add(new MenuItem() { Title = "React Native" });
            Head.Items.Add(subdItem1);
            Head.Items.Add(new MenuItem() { Title = "Web-Devlopment" });
            treehirmenu.Items.Add(Head);
        }
        public class MenuItem
        {
            public MenuItem()
            {
                this.Items = new ObservableCollection<menuitem>();
            }

            public string Title { get; set; }

            public ObservableCollection<menuitem> Items { get; set; }
        }
</menuitem></menuitem>

The following is a tree created by the previous code:


Screenshot_3: DataBinding TreeView

In the XAML markup, we mentioned the hierarchical data template for the TreeView item template. We instruct him to use the Items property to find the child's items by setting the template's Itemsource property, and within that, we define the actual template, which now consists only of a text block bound to the title property.

This first example was very simple, in fact so simple that we just added tree view objects manually, instead of generating a bunch of objects and then binding them. However, as things get a little more complicated, the benefits of using data bindings immediately become more apparent.

Wants to Talk with Our Highly Skilled WPF Developer? Contact Now

Multiple templates for different types

In the next example, we have taken a slightly more complex case, where we want to show the tree of families and their members. The family should be represented in one way, while each of its members should be shown in another way. We achieved this by creating two different templates and specifying them as the source of the tree (or window or application - which is really up to you) and then, allowing the tree view to select the appropriate template based on the type of data underlying it.

.Xaml file


  <grid margin="20">

        <treeview name="manyFamilies">
            <treeview.resources>

                <datatemplate x:key="MyFamily">
                    <stackpanel orientation="Horizontal">
                        <img height="868px" margin="0,0,7,0" src="/WpfApp3;component/Images/user.webp" width="382px">
                        <textblock text="{Binding Name}">

                        <textblock foreground="Green" he="" text=" (">
                        <textblock foreground="Green" text="{Binding Age}">
                        <textblock foreground="Green" text=" years)">
                    </textblock></textblock></textblock></textblock></stackpanel>
                </datatemplate>
            </treeview.resources>
        </treeview>
    </grid>

.cs File


  public MainWindow()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
            List<myfamily> families = new List<myfamily>();
            MyFamily familyone = new MyFamily() { Name = "The Doe's" };
            familyone.Members.Add(new FamilyMember() { Name = "John Doe", Age = 42 });
            familyone.Members.Add(new FamilyMember() { Name = "Jane Doe", Age = 39 });
            familyone.Members.Add(new FamilyMember() { Name = "Sammy Doe", Age = 13 });
            families.Add(familyone);

            MyFamily familyTwo = new MyFamily() { Name = "The Moe's" };
            familyTwo.Members.Add(new FamilyMember() { Name = "Mark Moe", Age = 31 });
            familyTwo.Members.Add(new FamilyMember() { Name = "Norma Moe", Age = 28 });
            familyTwo.Add(familyTwo);

            manyFamilies.ItemsSource = families;
        }

public class MyFamily
        {
            public MyFamily()
            {
                this.Members = new ObservableCollection<familymember>();
            }

            public string Name { get; set; }
            public ObservableCollection<familymember> Members { get; set; }
        }

        public class FamilyMember
        {
            public string Name { get; set; }
            public int Age { get; set; }
        }
</familymember></familymember></myfamily></myfamily>

Output


Reference: www.wpf-tutorial.com

As mentioned, two templates have been revealed as part of TreeView resources, allowing TreeView to select the appropriate template based on the type of data to be displayed. A specified sample for a MyFamily type is a hierarchical sample, in which the property of the members is used to show its MyFamily members.

The template defined for the MyFamily Member type is a regular data template, as there are no child members in this type. However, if we wanted each family member to have a collection of their children and possibly their children, we could use a hierarchical template instead.

In both samples, we used an image representing a family or family members, and then we show some interesting data about it, such as the number of family members or the age of the person.

In Code-Bank, we simply create two family patterns, fill each of them with a set of members, and then add each of the family to the list, which is then used as a source of items for the tree view.

Conclusion

While it is entirely possible to define a complete tree view using mark-up as we did in the examples above, this is not the best approach in most situations, and when you could code it later, this would have resulted in even more Code lines. Once again, the solution is data binding. Using data binding, the tree view is highly customizable and with the ability to specify multiple templates to represent different data types, the possibilities are almost endless.

原文:

A biblioteca patch-package é uma ferramenta muito útil para salvar alterações em bibliotecas de terceiros sem precisar fazer fork delas. Isso é especialmente útil quando você precisa fazer alterações em bibliotecas que são atualizadas frequentemente e você não quer perder essas alterações. Neste artigo, vamos ver como usar a biblioteca patch-package e como ela pode ser útil em diferentes situações.

Instalando e configurando o patch-package

Para usar o patch-package, você precisa instalá-lo primeiro usando npm ou yarn:

npm install patch-package

yarn add patch-package

Uma vez instalado, você precisa configurá-lo adicionando o seguinte script ao seu arquivo package.json:

"scripts": { "postinstall": "patch-package"

}

Isso fará com que o patch-package seja executado automaticamente após a instalação de dependências.

Salvando alterações

Agora que o patch-package está configurado, você pode fazer alterações na biblioteca de terceiros que você deseja salvar. Por exemplo, digamos que você queira alterar a cor de fundo de um componente em uma biblioteca de componentes de terceiros. Você pode fazer isso modificando o arquivo CSS da biblioteca.

Uma vez que você tenha feito as alterações, você pode usar o comando patch-package para salvar essas alterações:

npx patch-package nome-da-biblioteca

Isso cria um arquivo patch na pasta patches com as alterações que você fez. Você deve fazer o commit desse arquivo patch no seu repositório.

Aplicando alterações salvas

Quando você precisar atualizar a biblioteca, você pode usar o comando patch-package novamente para aplicar as alterações salvas. O patch-package irá comparar a versão atual da biblioteca com a versão salva e aplicar as alterações necessárias. Isso garante que as suas alterações não sejam perdidas durante atualizações futuras.

Para aplicar suas alterações use o seguinte comando:

npx patch-package

Exemplo de uso

Imagine que você está trabalhando em um projeto que usa a biblioteca react-datepicker. Durante o desenvolvimento, você percebe que o componente não possui a funcionalidade de selecionar múltiplas datas. Você decide fazer essa adição por conta própria, modificando o código fonte da biblioteca.

Uma vez que você tenha feito as alterações, você pode usar o comando patch-package para salvar essas alterações:

npx patch-package react-datepicker

Isso cria um arquivo patch na pasta patches com as alterações que você fez. Você deve fazer o commit desse arquivo patch no seu repositório.

Quando a biblioteca react-datepicker for atualizada, você pode usar o comando patch-package novamente para aplicar as alterações salvas e garantir que a funcionalidade de seleção de múltiplas datas continue funcionando.

Em resumo, a biblioteca patch-package é uma ferramenta muito útil para salvar alterações em bibliotecas de terceiros sem precisar fazer fork delas. Ela permite que você faça alterações em bibliotecas que são atualizadas frequentemente e garante que essas alterações não sejam perdidas durante atualizações futuras. Isso facilita muito o gerenciamento de dependências em projetos, pois permite que você faça alterações necessárias sem precisar criar forks desnecessários.

假设您要创建一个对象中所有值的数组。 肯定有不止一种方法可以做到这一点。 我们将在本文中探讨几种不同的方法。

此方法接受一个对象。 它返回数组中对象的可枚举属性值。

让我们看一个例子：

 const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = Object.values(obj); console.log(valuesArr);

正如您在示例中看到的，我们将obj对象传递给了Object.values方法。 返回值被分配给valuesArr 。

这是valuesArr的值：

 [ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor" ]

让我们来看看我们可以做到这一点的不同方式。

另一种创建对象中所有值的数组的方法是使用for...in语句。 该声明一直追溯到 ES5，因此，它已经存在了一段时间。 此语句迭代对象的可枚举字符串属性，不包括符号属性。

使用之前的相同对象，让我们看一个示例：

 const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = []; for (const prop in obj) { valuesArr.push(obj[prop]); } console.log(valuesArr);

在示例中，我们声明了一个空数组valuesArr 。 然后，我们使用for...in语句迭代对象obj的属性。 因此，我们可以像这样使用它来访问每个属性的值： obj[prop] 。 然后我们将每个值推入valuesArr数组。

以下是valuesArr的值：

 [ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor" ]

让我们看看另一种创建对象中所有值的数组的方法。

此静态方法返回数组中对象可枚举属性的键。 我们可以使用Array.map方法来帮助获取每个属性的值。 让我们来看一个例子：

 const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = Object.keys(obj).map(key => obj[key]) console.log(valuesArr);

在示例中，我们链接到Array.map方法，该方法接收对象obj中每个属性的键。 我们使用键来访问每个属性的值，就像我们在for...in语句中所做的那样。 这些值被返回并分配给valuesArr 。

让我们看一下valuesArr的值：

 [ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor" ]

结论

正如我在介绍中所说，在 JavaScript 中创建对象中所有值的数组的方法不止一种。 我没有深入了解这些方法的性能，但如果您对此感兴趣，请在下方发表评论。 如果我没有涵盖您首选的执行此操作的方式，请在下面的评论中分享。

感谢您的阅读，直到下一次。

原文:

Suppose you want to create an array of all the values in an object. There is definitely more than one way to do that. We'll look at a few different approaches in this post.

Object.values() method

The Object.values static method was released in ECMASript 2017 aka ES8. This method accepts an object. It returns the objects enumberable property's values in an array.

Let's look at an example:

const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = Object.values(obj); console.log(valuesArr);

As you can see in the example, we passed in the obj object to the Object.values method. The returned value was assigned to valuesArr.

And here's the value of valuesArr:

[ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor"
]

Let's take a look at different way we could do this.

for...in statement

Another way to create an array of all the values in an object is to use the for...in statement. The statement goes all the way back to ES5, so, it's been around for a bit. This statement iterates over the enumerable String properties of an obect with the exclusion of the symbol properties.

Using the same object from earlier, let's check out an example:

const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = []; for (const prop in obj) { valuesArr.push(obj[prop]);
} console.log(valuesArr);

In the example we declare an empty array, valuesArr. We then use the for...in statement to iterate over the properties of our object, obj. The declaration, const prop in the for...in statement is actually the string property name of each property. Therefore, we can use that to access the value of each property like so: obj[prop]. We then push each value in to the valuesArr array.

And here are the values for valuesArr:

[ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor"
]

Let's check out one more way to create an array of all the values in an object.

Object.keys() method

The Object.keys static method was has been around since ES5. This static method returns the objects enumberable property's keys in an array. We can use the Array.map method to help is get the value of each property. Let's jump in to an example:

const obj = { a: 'hello', b: 'world', c: 'lorem', d: 'ipsum', e: 'dolor' }; const valuesArr = Object.keys(obj).map(key => obj[key]) console.log(valuesArr);

In the example we are chaining on the Array.map method which receives the key of each property in our object, obj. We use the key to access the value of each property, just like we did in the for...in statement. The values are returned and assgned to valuesArr.

Let's have a look at the value of valuesArr:

[ "hello", "world", "lorem", "ipsum", "dolor"
]

Conclusion

As I said in the intro, there's more than one way to create an array of all the values in an object in JavaScript. I didn't get in to the performance of these methods, but if that's something you'd be interested in, leave a comment below. If I didn't cover your preferred way of doing this, please share in a comment below.

Thank you for reading, until next time.

Items.Title: 2023 年每个人都必须知道的 10 个重要的 JavaScript 对象方法 | 10 Important JavaScript Object Methods everyone must know in 2023
Items.Description:
它们是与对象关联的函数，可以在该对象上使用点符号或括号符号调用。 在 JavaScript 中，对象可以有属性和方法。 属性是数据变量，方法是可以通过调用它们来执行的函数。

对象方法用于对对象和/或其属性执行某些操作或操作。

在 JavaScript 中，对象具有某些内置方法，可用于操作对象的属性和值并与之交互。 一些最常用的对象方法包括：

对象键

此数组仅包含可枚举的属性。 可枚举属性是那些内部可枚举标志设置为 true 的属性。

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" }; let keys = Object.keys(obj); console.log(keys); // Output: [ "name", "age", "city" ]

对象值

该数组仅包含可枚举属性的值。

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" }; let values = Object.values(obj); console.log(values); // Output: [ "John", 30, "New York" ]

对象条目

该数组仅包含可枚举的属性。

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" }; let entries = Object.entries(obj); console.log(entries); // Output: [ ["name", "John"], ["age", 30], ["city", "New York"] ]

对象分配

此方法用于将属性从一个对象复制到另一个对象，它会完整复制所有属性，包括不可枚举的属性。

例如：

 let obj1 = { name: "John" }; let obj2 = { age: 30 }; let obj3 = Object.assign(obj1, obj2); console.log(obj3); // Output: { name: "John", age: 30 }

对象定义属性

例如：

 let obj = {}; Object.defineProperty(obj, "name", { value: "John", writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true }); console.log(obj.name); // Output: "John"

对象定义属性

例如：

 let obj = {}; Object.defineProperties(obj, { name: { value: "John", writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true }, age: { value: 30, writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true } }); console.log(obj.name); // Output: "John" console.log(obj.age); // Output: 30

对象 getOwnPropertyNames

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John" }; let descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, "name"); console.log(descriptor); // Output: { // value: "John", // writable: true, // enumerable: true, // configurable: true // }

对象 getOwnPropertyNames

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John", age: 30 }; let properties = Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj); console.log(properties); // Output: [ "name", "age" ]

对象 getPrototypeOf

例如：

 let obj = { name: "John" }; let prototype = Object.getPrototypeOf(obj); console.log(prototype); // Output: {}

对象创建

如果原型不是对象或 null，它会抛出 TypeError。

例如：

 let prototype = { sayHi: function() { console.log("Hi") } }; let obj = Object.create(prototype); obj.sayHi(); // Output: "Hi"

这些方法允许您以更强大的方式与 JavaScript 中的对象进行交互，它们允许您创建、修改和检索对象的属性和值，以及操作对象的原型链。

原文:

JavaScript object methods are actions that can be performed on JavaScript objects. They are functions that are associated with an object and can be invoked using the dot notation or bracket notation on that object. In JavaScript, objects can have properties and methods. Properties are data variables and methods are functions that can be executed by invoking them.

Object methods are used to perform certain actions or operations on an object and/or its properties.

In JavaScript, objects have certain built-in methods that can be used to manipulate and interact with the object’s properties and values. Some of the most commonly used object methods include:

Object Keys

Object.keys(obj): Returns an array of the object's own enumerable properties. The Object.keys() the method returns an array of a given object's own enumerable property names, in the same order as we get with a normal loop. This array only includes properties that are enumerable. Enumerable properties are those properties whose internal enumerable flag is set to true.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" };
let keys = Object.keys(obj);
console.log(keys); // Output: [ "name", "age", "city" ]

Object Values

Object.values(obj): Returns an array of the object's own enumerable property values. The Object.values() the method returns an array of a given object's own enumerable property values, in the same order as that provided by a for...in the loop. This array includes only the values of enumerable properties.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" };
let values = Object.values(obj);
console.log(values); // Output: [ "John", 30, "New York" ]

Object Entries

Object.entries(obj): Returns an array of the object's own enumerable property [key, value] pairs. The Object.entries() the method returns an array of a given object's own enumerable property [key, value] pairs, in the same order as that provided by a for...in loop. This array includes only the properties that are enumerable.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John", age: 30, city: "New York" };
let entries = Object.entries(obj);
console.log(entries); // Output: [ ["name", "John"], ["age", 30], ["city", "New York"] ]

Object assign

Object.assign(obj1, obj2, ...): Copies the values of all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object and returns the target object. This method is used to copy properties from one object to another, it makes a complete copy of all properties, including non-enumerable properties.

For example:

let obj1 = { name: "John" };
let obj2 = { age: 30 };
let obj3 = Object.assign(obj1, obj2);
console.log(obj3); // Output: { name: "John", age: 30 }

Object defineProperty

Object.defineProperty(obj, prop, descriptor): Defines a new property directly on an object, or modifies an existing property on an object, and returns the object. The defineProperty() the method allows you to define a new property on an object, or modify an existing property, and provides a way to set the property's attributes.

For example:

let obj = {};
Object.defineProperty(obj, "name", {
  value: "John",
  writable: true,
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: true
});
console.log(obj.name); // Output: "John"

Object defineProperties

Object.defineProperties(obj, props): Defines new or modifies existing properties directly on an object and returns the object. The defineProperties() the method is similar to the defineProperty() method, but it allows you to define multiple properties at once, using an object that contains the property descriptors.

For example:

let obj = {};
Object.defineProperties(obj, {
  name: {
    value: "John",
    writable: true,
    enumerable: true,
    configurable: true
  },
  age: {
    value: 30,
    writable: true,
    enumerable: true,
    configurable: true
  }
});
console.log(obj.name); // Output: "John"
console.log(obj.age); // Output: 30

Object getOwnPropertyNames

Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, prop): Returns a property descriptor for the own property of an object. The getOwnPropertyDescriptor() method returns a property descriptor for an own property (that is, one directly present on an object, not present by virtue of being along an object's prototype chain) of a given object.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John" };
let descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, "name");
console.log(descriptor); 
// Output: { 
//    value: "John",
//    writable: true,
//    enumerable: true,
//    configurable: true 
// }

Object getOwnPropertyNames

Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj): Returns an array of all own properties' names (enumerable or non-enumerable) of an object. The getOwnPropertyNames() method returns an array of all properties (enumerable or non-enumerable) found directly on an object.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John", age: 30 };
let properties = Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj);
console.log(properties); // Output: [ "name", "age" ]

Object getPrototypeOf

Object.getPrototypeOf(obj): Returns the prototype (i.e. the internal [[Prototype]]) of the specified object. The getPrototypeOf() method returns the prototype (i.e. the value of the internal [[Prototype]] property) of the specified object.

For example:

let obj = { name: "John" };
let prototype = Object.getPrototypeOf(obj);
console.log(prototype); // Output: {}

Object Create

Object.create(proto[, propertiesObject]): creates an object with the given prototype or with null as a prototype if none is given, and an optional properties object that describes its own properties. The create() method creates a new object with the specified prototype object and properties. If the prototype is not an object or null, it throws a TypeError.

For example:

let prototype = { sayHi: function() { console.log("Hi") } };
let obj = Object.create(prototype);
obj.sayHi(); // Output: "Hi"

These methods allow you to interact with objects in JavaScript in a more powerful way, they allow you to create, modify, and retrieve properties and values of objects, and to manipulate the prototype chain of an object.

Items.Title: 学习前端 Web 开发的最佳方式 | The Best Way to Learn Frontend Web Development
Items.Description:
前端开发是 Web 开发的一个重要方面，因为它负责网站的视觉和交互元素。 学习前端开发可能是一项艰巨的任务，但如果方法正确，它可以是一种愉快而有益的体验。 以下是学习前端开发的一些技巧：

从基础开始：在深入研究复杂的框架和库之前，必须对 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 有扎实的理解。 这些都是前端开发的基石，掌握它们会更容易学习更高级的概念。

练习、练习、再练习：学习前端开发的最好方法就是亲自动手。 构建小型项目并尝试不同的技术。 这将帮助您了解不同元素如何协同工作以及如何解决问题。

向他人学习：前端开发社区庞大而充满活力，在线提供许多资源。 加入在线论坛、阅读博客和观看教程以向其他开发人员学习并获得新的视角。

通过构建学习：与其仅仅阅读新技术或框架，不如尝试用它构建一些东西。 这将帮助您了解它在实践中是如何工作的，并给您带来成就感。

保持最新：前端开发在不断发展，新技术和技巧不断涌现。 通过关注行业领导者和参与社区，了解最新动态。

边做边学：学习前端开发的最佳方式是参与真实世界的项目。 您将学习如何作为团队的一员工作、如何管理您的时间和资源以及如何与他人沟通。

总之，前端开发是一个令人兴奋的领域，需要耐心、努力和奉献。 从基础开始，练习，向他人学习并了解最新技术。 请记住，学习是一段旅程，每一步都会让您更接近目标。



原文:

Front-end development is a crucial aspect of web development, as it is responsible for the visual and interactive elements of a website. Learning front-end development can be a daunting task, but with the right approach, it can be an enjoyable and rewarding experience. Here are some tips for learning front-end development:

Start with the basics: Before diving into complex frameworks and libraries, it's essential to have a solid understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. These are the building blocks of front-end development, and mastering them will make it easier to learn more advanced concepts.

Practice, practice, practice: The best way to learn front-end development is by doing it. Build small projects and experiment with different techniques. This will help you understand how different elements work together and how to troubleshoot issues.

Learn from others: The front-end development community is vast and vibrant, with many resources available online. Join online forums, read blogs, and watch tutorials to learn from other developers and gain new perspectives.

Learn by building: Instead of just reading about a new technique or framework, try building something with it. This will help you understand how it works in practice and give you a sense of accomplishment.

Stay up to date: Front-end development is constantly evolving, with new technologies and techniques emerging all the time. Stay up to date with the latest developments by following industry leaders and participating in the community.

Learn by doing: The best way to learn front-end development is by working on real-world projects. You'll learn how to work as part of a team, how to manage your time and resources, and how to communicate with others.

In conclusion, front-end development is an exciting field that requires patience, hard work and dedication. Start with the basics, practice, learn from others and stay up to date with the latest technologies. Remember, learning is a journey, and every step takes you closer to your goal.

Items.Title: 选择合适的云服务提供商：要考虑的 8 大因素 | Choose the Right Cloud Service Provider: Top 8 Factors to Consider
Items.Description:
如何选择合适的云服务商？ 你有同样的问题吗？ 您想知道可以帮助您选择合适的云服务提供商的因素吗？ 如果这些问题的答案是肯定的，那么您来对地方了。 我们需要了解，没有用于雇用云服务提供商的通用框架，因为每个云服务提供商 (CSP) 都各不相同。 市场上充斥着不同的 CSP，每个提供商（Microsoft、Amazon、Salesforce、Google）都提供支持业务运营的无数服务。 在这种困惑中，有一个问题仍然没有答案，那就是，您如何选择合适的云提供商？

答案很简单，就是了解独特的业务需求并通过定义的选择流程选择合适的云服务提供商。

Types of Cloud Computing Services Delivery Models

软件即服务 (SaaS)

  • 电子信息管理系统
  • 事件
  • 环境健康安全
  • 任务
  • 浪费 SaaS 提供商是通过 Internet 托管和提供服务的提供商。 这些服务包括 CRM、HRM、数据管理软件、生产力等技术。

平台即服务 (SaaS)

  • 应用程序开发
  • 讯息
  • 仪表板
  • 集成 在 PaaS 基础架构中，用户可以访问和执行软件开发过程中的相关功能。 在此模型中，云无法控制其基础设施。

基础架构即服务 (IaaS)

  • 联网
  • 安全
  • 系统管理
  • 可扩展性 在此模型中，企业获得交付的基础设施组件，这些组件存在于本地数据中心。

选择云服务提供商之前要考虑的 8个方面 以下几点将帮助您有效地选择合适的云服务提供商。

证书和标准认可

每当您开始寻找合适的云合作伙伴时，请确保他们符合公认的行业标准和质量框架，并遵循最佳实践。 这将是一个开始，您可以将潜在的提供商列入候选名单。 您可以在提供商中寻找的一些标准和认证包括 DMTF、ISO、NIST、OASIS、IEEE、IETF、SNIA 等。 服务与技术 您的业务目标、云环境以及技术与服务之间的和谐提供商提供的是必须的。 确保工作负载和管理偏好非常适合提供商遵循的体系结构、服务和标准。 在聘请云解决方案咨询提供商之前，确定使工作负载合适所需的重新编码或定制级别。 除此之外，您还应该向您的 CSP 询问他们的服务开发路线图。 了解他们的想法，看看他们的路线图是否符合您的长期业务需求。 如果您正在寻找 SaaS 服务提供商，您应该在最终决定之前考虑功能、服务和集成路线图。

数据管理与数据安全

让我们先谈谈数据管理。 数据是新的石油，其管理对业务至关重要。 确保您的暂定云服务提供商对其数据中心位置保持透明。 存储、处理和管理数据的位置在 CSP 的选择中起着至关重要的作用。 确保他们为您提供满足您的要求和义务的数据选择和控制。 数据安全也是选​​择合适的云服务咨询提供商的一个非常重要的因素。 彻底分析他们的数据和系统安全级别、安全操作的成熟度以及他们的安全治理流程。 确保提供商根据您的业务安全政策和程序进行信息安全控制。

您还可以向您的 CSP 索取安全审计报告、补救措施的证据以及他们之前工作过的项目的事件报告。

伙伴关系和服务依赖性几乎每个企业都希望获得完美和及时的服务，并寻求值得投资的价值。 因此，分析 CSP 的可用性、容量、响应时间和支持服务变得至关重要。

企业必须与服务提供商签订云服务水平协议，即 SLA。 它有助于与他们建立透明的合同关系。 遵守 GDPR 法规、对行为准则的明确说明以及服务依赖性是在选择合适的云服务提供商之前需要考虑的其他一些重要因素。

服务交付所有要点都应在服务和可交付成果方面的协议中适当提及。 正确理解与服务相关的角色和职责，以及它们在服务提供者和服务接受者之间的分配。

服务定义、角色和职责、服务管理、服务可用性以及灾难恢复和服务连续性是成功交付服务的一些最重要因素。

合同和 SLA仍然有许多云提供商使用不必要的复杂且以技术行话为中心的合同，这些合同可能具有误导性并且无法帮助企业做出公平的决定。 永远不要忽视复杂的条款和条件。 始终要求您的 CSP 提供有弹性的条款，并了解他们支持所提供的灵活性的方式。

以下是签订合同时要考虑的关键因素。

数据保证

  • 数据管理
  • 数据转换
  • 数据安全，以及
  • 所有权和使用权

商务条款

  • 保险
  • 公示
  • 运营审查
  • 费用和商业条款，以及
  • 商业政策

法律保护

  • 保证书
  • 知识产权
  • 赔偿，和
  • 责任限制

灾难备份和数据恢复
拥有云服务平台的企业不必担心数据丢失或被盗。 云技术在高度加密的环境中保护数据。 它可以保护数据免受各种灾难的影响，例如自然灾害、停电等。值得信赖的云服务提供商向其客户保证，他们的信息将得到强有力的保护，免受各种不可预见的自然和人为威胁。

每当您聘请云服务提供商时，请放心他们的数据备份和恢复能力。

成本最后但绝对不是最不重要的。 在选择合适的云服务提供商时，必须考虑这个因素。 在做出业务决策时，成本因素起着至关重要的作用。 市场上云服务供应商种类繁多，不要被诱人的低成本解决方案所迷惑。 低成本似乎很重要，但在这种情况下受骗的可能性很高。 这并不意味着您应该避免考虑低成本的云服务。 请保持警惕，不要落入任何骗局。

您可以选择主要三种不同的成本模型。 这些模型是——

  • 现收现付 -
    在这个模块中，您必须为使用的数据存储空间的大小付费。 没有义务支付前期费用。

  • 预订的 -
    您可以购买一定的云空间并将其作为特定时间的备用存储空间。 此模块需要预付费用。

  • 批量折扣 –
    同一个云服务提供商会根据您的业务需求为您提供不同的服务。 随着业务的增长，服务的数量也会增加。

确保您选择合适的云服务提供商的标准——奖励积分

  • 评估技术和运营能力
  • 迁移支持、供应商锁定和退出计划
  • 性能和可靠性
  • 服务交付
  • 数据政策和保护
  • 商业条款
  • 法律保护
  • 服务等级协定
  • 云端广告
  • 服务依赖
  • 分包商和服务依赖

结论在这篇文章中，我们包含了所有重要因素。 作为企业，您在选择合适的云服务提供商之前应该考虑上述因素。 我们建议您验证云服务提供商提供的认证和标准。 始终建立可行的 SLA 和合同条款。

如果您对所讨论的任何一点有任何疑问，您可以聘请一家优秀的云服务提供商，他们的专业指导将帮助您完成从云服务选择到部署的整个过程。

谢谢你。


感谢您访问 DEV，我们一直在努力培养这个伟大的社区，并希望您能加入我们。 如果您想创建一个帐户，可以在此处注册。

原文:

How to choose the right cloud service provider? Are you having the same question in mind? Do you want to know the factors that can help you choose the right cloud service provider? If the answer to these questions is yes, you have arrived at the right place. We need to understand there’s no common framework for hiring a cloud service provider as every Cloud Service Provider (CSP) is different from the other. The market is filled with different CSPs, and each provider (Microsoft, Amazon, Salesforce, Google) is offering myriad services ranging that support business operation. Amidst this confusion, one question remains answered, and it is, how do you select the right cloud provider?

The answer is simple, and it is by understanding the unique business needs and selecting the right cloud service provider through a defined selection process.

Types of Cloud Computing Services Delivery Models

Software as a Service (SaaS)

  • EIM
  • Incidents
  • EHS
  • Tasks
  • Waste A SaaS provider is one that host and provides service through the internet. These services include technologies like CRM, HRM, Data Management Software, Productivity, etc.

Platform as a Service (SaaS)

  • App Development
  • Messaging
  • Dashboard
  • Integration In the PaaS infrastructure, users get to access & perform relevant functions that come under the software development process. Clouds have no control over their infrastructure in this model.

Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS)

  • Networking
  • Security
  • System Management
  • Scalability In this model, the business gets delivered infrastructure components that are present in the on-premise data center.

8 Areas to Consider Before you Pick Cloud Service Provider The following points will help you effectively choose the right Cloud Service Provider.

Certificates and Standard Recognition

Whenever you start your hunt to choose the right cloud partner make sure they comply with recognized industry standards & quality framework and follow best practices. It will be a start, and you can shortlist the potential providers. Some of the standards and certifications that you can look for in a provider include DMTF, ISO, NIST, OASIS, IEEE, IETF, SNIA, etc. Services & Technologies The harmony between your business objective, the cloud environment, and the technologies & services offered by the provider is a must. Make sure the workloads and management preferences are well suited to the architectures, services, and standards followed by the provider. Before you hire a cloud solution consulting provider, identify the level of re-coding or customization required to make the workload suitable. Along with this, you should also ask your CSP about their service development roadmap. Understand their thinking and see if their roadmap fits with your business requirements in the long run. If you are looking for SaaS service providers, you should consider features, services and integration roadmap before the final decision.

Data Management & Data Security

Let’s first talk about data management. Data is the new oil, and its management is crucial for business. Make sure your tentative cloud service provider remains transparent about their data center locations. The location where your data gets stored, processed, and managed plays a crucial role in the selection of CSP. Make sure they provide you with the choice & control of your data that meets your requirements and obligations. Data Security is also a highly important factor to choose the right cloud service consulting provider. Thoroughly analyze their data & system security level, the maturity of security operations, and also their security governance processes. Make sure the provider has the information security control as per your business security policies and procedures.

You can also ask your CSPs for security audit reports, evidence of remedial actions and the incident reports for the project on which they have worked previously.

Partnerships and Service Dependencies Almost every business wants perfect and prompt service and seeks values worth invested money. Therefore, it becomes essential to analyze CSPs availability, capacity, response time, and support services.

A business must have Cloud Service Level Agreements a.k.a SLAs with the service provider. It helps in establishing transparent contractual relationships with them. Compliance with the GDPR regulations, explicit clarification on the code of practice, and service dependencies are some of the other important factors to consider before selecting the right Cloud Service Provider.

Service Delivery All the points should be properly mentioned in an agreement in terms of services and deliverables. Have a proper understanding of the roles and responsibilities related to the services along with their distribution between the service provider and service takers.

Service definition, roles & responsibilities, service management, service availability, and DR & service continuity are some of the most important factors that make a successful service delivery.

Contracts and SLAs There are still many cloud providers who are using unnecessary complex and technical jargon-centric contracts that could be misleading and don’t help businesses make a fair decision. Never ignore complex terms and conditions. Always ask your CSPs to offer resilient terms and also understand the way they will support the offered flexibilities.

The following are key factors to consider while making contracts.

Data Assurance

  • Data Management
  • Data Conversion
  • Data Security, and
  • Ownership and Use Rights

Business Terms

  • Insurance
  • Publicity
  • Operational Reviews
  • Fees and Commercial Terms, and
  • Business Policies

Legal Protection

  • Warranties
  • Intellectual Property
  • Indemnification, and
  • Limitation of Liability

Disaster Backup and Data Recovery
CRM solutions such as Salesforce consulting services have a provision for data recovery. Businesses with cloud service platforms do not have to worry about data loss or theft. Cloud technology keeps data secured in a highly encrypted environment. It keeps data safe from all types of disasters, such as natural disasters, power failure, etc. A trustworthy cloud service provider assures its customers a strong protection of their information from all sorts of unforeseen natural & man-made threats.

Whenever you hire a Cloud service provider, rest assured for their data backup and recovery capabilities.

Cost Last but definitely not the least. This factor is imperative to consider while selecting the right cloud service provider. The cost factor plays a crucial role when it comes to making a business decision. There are different kinds of cloud service providers in the market, do not fall for the tempting low-cost solutions. Low cost seems to be a great deal, but the chances of deception are high in such cases. It does not mean that you should avoid considering low-cost cloud services. Just be vigilant to not fall in the prey of any scam.

There are majorly three different kinds of cost models which you can choose. These models are –

  • Pay as you Go –
    In this module, you have to pay for the size of the data storage space used. There is no obligation to pay the upfront cost.

  • Reserved –
    You can buy definite cloud space and keep it as a reserve storage space for a particular time. There will be an upfront cost for this module.

  • Volume Discounts –
    The same cloud service provider offers you different services as per your business requirements. As your business grows, the number of services also increases.

Criteria to Ensure You Select The Right Cloud Service Provider – Bonus Points

  • Assess technical & operational capabilities
  • Migration support, vendor lock-in, and exit planning
  • Performance and reliability
  • Service delivery
  • Data policies and protection
  • Business terms
  • Legal protections
  • Service level agreements
  • Cloud commercials
  • Service dependencies
  • Subcontractors & service dependencies

Conclusion In this post, we have included all the important factors. You, as a business should consider the above discussed factors before choosing the right cloud service provider. We suggest you validate both certifications and standards that the cloud service provider offers. Always establish workable SLAs & contractual terms.

If you have any doubt related to any of the discussed points, you hire a good cloud service provider, and their professional guidance will help you through the process ranging from cloud service selection to deployment.

Thank you.

托马斯雷吉

发表于 1 月 23 日

这是本文讨论的想法的实现：提出一种编写 JavaScript 的新方法。 我创建了一个概念VSCode Deno Notebook 。

作为一名全栈开发人员，我以前没有使用过像交互式计算那样的“笔记本”。 它们主要用于数据科学以运行查询和可视化数据。 但是，我在类似的东西中看到了巨大的实用性。 能够随心所欲地在较小的代码库子集中执行代码。 我最近一直沉迷于一般如何组织代码。 在同一个地方同时看到一个函数和您编写的测试不是很好吗？ 也许甚至还可以进行测试。 一般来说，基于接口的测试运行器自 IDE 出现以来就已经存在，但测试通常与通常在运行时运行的代码分开，放入它们自己的文件中。

在 JavaScript 生态系统中，通常有多种方法可以做某事，同样的事情也适用于编写代码本身。 例如，您可能希望将所有函数分解到它们自己的文件中，或者拥有将常见或相似函数连接在一起的类。 如何组织代码的选择取决于手头的具体任务。 我发现如果我们根本不必处理文件抽象可能会更容易。

我创建了一个新的原型 VSCode 扩展，允许在一个笔记本中创建多个函数。 每个“单元格”都是一个函数，导出为自己的文件并可以独立使用。 此扩展可以执行常规文件和测试。 单元测试可以保存并在单元内运行，并使用deno test命令执行，而常规脚本将使用deno run 。 扩展通过第一个注释检测文件类型，该注释充当生成文件的名称。 总之，作为一名全栈开发人员，我看到了在我的工作流程中使用交互式笔记本的潜力。 在较小的代码库子集中执行代码和运行测试，并在同一位置直观地查看函数和测试的能力非常有用。 我邀请其他人查看我的存储库并分享他们对如何进一步改进和构建的想法。 这是回购协议的链接。

这是概念验证（全文： dev.to/reggi/vscode-deno-notebook-48kp ）

要测试扩展，请执行以下操作：

  1. 运行 npm 安装
  2. 转到 VSCode 中的“调试”和“运行扩展”
  3. 等待本地 vscode 窗口弹出（这有扩展运行）
  4. 导航到notebook文件夹
  5. 打开example.denote文件

Thomas Reggi

Posted on Jan 23

This is the implementation of the ideas discussed in this article here: Proposing a new way to write JavaScript..

TLDR; I created a concept VSCode Deno Notebook.

As a full-stack developer, I haven't used "notebooks" like the interactive computing kind before. They are mainly used in data science to run queries and visualize data. However, I see immense utility in something like that. The ability to execute code within a smaller subset of the codebase on a whim. I've been obsessed lately about how to organize code in general. Wouldn't it be nice to visually see both a function and the test you write in the same place? Perhaps even run a test also. Interface-based test runners have existed since the beginning of IDEs in general, but tests are often separated into their own file apart from the code that would normally run at runtime.

In the JavaScript ecosystem, there are often many ways to do something, the same thing applies to authoring code itself. For example, you may want to break all your functions down into their own files, or have classes that join common or similar functions together. The choice of how to organize code depends on the specific task at hand. I find that it may be easier if we didn't have to deal with the file abstraction at all.

I have created a new prototype VSCode extension that allows for creating multiple functions in one notebook. Each "cell" is a function that is exported as its own file and can be used independently. This extension can execute both regular files as well as tests. Unit tests can be saved and run within the cell, and executed with the deno test command, while a regular script will use deno run. The extension detects the type of file by the first comment, which acts as the name for the generated file. AST parsing could be used here for name and test detection in the future.

In conclusion, as a full-stack developer, I see the potential of using interactive notebooks in my workflow. The ability to execute code and run tests within a smaller subset of the codebase, and visually see both a function and the test in the same place, is extremely useful. I invite others to check out my repository and share their thoughts on how this can be further improved and built upon. Here's a link to the repo.

This is a proof of concept (full article: dev.to/reggi/vscode-deno-notebook-48kp)

To test the extension do the following:

  1. Run npm install
  2. Go to "debug" and "Run Extension" in VSCode
  3. Wait for the local vscode window to popup (this has the extension running)
  4. Navigate to the notebook folder
  5. Open the example.denote file

Items.Description:
萨米·乌拉·萨利姆

发表于 1 月 23 日

两种类型的存储数据

  1. 在磁盘上

  2. 在记忆中

  3. 在磁盘上
    我们存储在磁盘上的数据将是持久性的。 这意味着服务器宕机后数据仍然可用。

  4. 在记忆中
    这里的数据不是持久化的，因为只要服务器启动，数据就可用。 一旦服务器关闭，数据就会被删除。

综上所述，我们可以说存储涉及三个概念。

我们将在本系列的未来研究更多概念。
谢谢你！

Items.Title: 如何对待你的追随者 | How to Treat Your Followers
Items.Description:
你好忠实的博客读者，

你们都好吗？ 所以今天的博客将重点关注我如何处理我正在创建的社交媒体应用程序的“关注者”。 在开始使用该应用程序之前，我已经了解到关注者是通过在两个用户（“关注者”和“user_they_follow”）之间创建一个连接表来创建的。 我可以在 Internet 上找到各种文章，这些文章引导我了解如何在我的 Follow 模型中创建这种关系——这篇文章就是一个很好的例子（ betterprogramming.pub/how-to-create-af…）——但我将重点介绍在建立基本关系后可以用来对待追随者的一些方式。

由于社交媒体上的大多数人都痴迷于查看他们在任何给定时间有多少粉丝，我知道我可以将这一重要信息留在后端。 我需要在页面上显示这些详细信息，以便使用我的神话人物可以互相吹嘘他们的追随者。 所以，我需要创建一些方法。 方法 #1 非常简单——我想显示每个用户在他们的主页上有多少关注者。

为此，我只需要在用户模型中创建一个简单的方法：

 def total_followers self.followers.length end

在这里，我只是获取当前用户并通过 has_many 关系访问他们拥有的所有关注者，然后使用 .length 获取这些关注者的总数。 然后我将 total_followers 添加到 user_serializer 属性，并在前面显示 user.total_followers，我有一个非常简单的方法来满足应用程序上的自恋者。

我创建了一个同样简单的方法来计算用户使用方法 #2 关注的人数：

 def total_users_you_follow self.users_they_follow.length end

现在，人们仅仅知道有多少人在关​​注他们是不够的——他们还想知道谁在关注他们。 因此，为此我回到了我的用户模型并创建了方法#3：

 def list_followers self.followers.map do |follower| follower.username end end

在本例中，我获取当前用户并映射所有关注者并返回这些关注者的所有用户名的数组。 现在，稍后，我可能想传递的不仅仅是名字，也许还想设置一个新的序列化器，这样我就可以发送更多的关注者数据来显示，但由于这个项目必须在两天内上交，我我现在只想处理名字。 我通过将以下内容发布到页面 user.list_followers.toString() 来将名称作为字符串发布。

再一次，我只需要稍微改变一下就可以收集用户关注的相同信息和方法#4：

 def list_users_you_follow self.users_they_follow.map do |followed| followed.username end end

好的，一切正常，但我还有一个问题想解决。 每个用户页面都有一个“关注我”按钮，单击该按钮会将信息发送到后端，并在下表中输入关注者和他们关注的人的 user_id，但目前正面没有任何指示结束，任何事情都发生了。 所以我想设置一个三元组，如果当前用户不关注他们正在查看其页面的人，则显示“关注我”，如果他们不关注该人，则显示“你关注”。 那么...我该如何实现呢？

我的第一个想法是另一种方法，因为我很喜欢这些方法，但后来我意识到我可以进一步利用我刚刚编写的几个方法。

在 UserPage.js 组件中，我声明了一个新的布尔变量：

 let alreadyFollow = false

在该组件中，我已经有一个名为 userPageInfo 的变量，其中包含该页面所在用户的所有信息。 因此，我可以使用 list_followers 方法获取该用户的所有关注者，然后搜索它们：

 userPageInfo.list_followers.forEach((fol) => {

然后我可以将每个关注者的用户名与当前登录用户的用户名进行比较

 if (fol === user.username)

然后，如果我找到匹配项，我只需将已经遵循的变量更改为 true。 似乎很容易。 让我们检查一下它是否有效。

 TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'forEach')

快点！！ 为什么那行不通？ 哦...我刚刚意识到我当前所在的用户页面是一个没有任何关注者的用户，因此 forEach 试图通过任何内容进行映射并遇到错误。 我可以解决这个问题！...我希望。

我只需要将 forEach 循环包装在另一个 if 语句中，我将再次调用我已经编写的方法之一。

 if (userPageInfo.total_followers > 0)

所以现在，forEach 将仅在用户有关注者时运行，如果没有，则 alreadyFollow 布尔值将根据需要保持 false。 让我们来看看...

成功！！ 所以这是 if 语句的最终版本，forEach 循环。

 if (userPageInfo.total_followers > 0) { userPageInfo.list_followers.forEach((fol) => { if (fol === user.username) { alreadyFollow = true console.log(alreadyFollow) } }) }

这是我用来在页面上放置正确关注按钮的三元组：

 <h5> {alreadyFollow ? <button> You Follow {userPageInfo.username} </button> : <button onClick={handleFollow}>Follow {userPageInfo.username}</button>} </h5>

完美的。 所以现在每个人都知道谁在关注谁，我会留给他们找出原因。



原文:

Hello loyal blog readers,

How are you both doing? So today's blog is going to focus on how I'm dealing with "followers" for the social media app that I'm creating. Before I started on the app, I already understood that followers are created by making a join table between two users - "the follower" and the "user_they_follow". There were a variety of articles I was able to find on the internet that walked me through how to create that relationship in my Follow model - this one serves as a good example (betterprogramming.pub/how-to-create-a-f…) - but I'm going to focus on some ways you can treat your followers after you've created that basic relationship.

Since most people on social media are obsessed with seeing how many followers they have at any given time, I knew I could just leave this important information sitting in the backend. I needed to get those details displayed on the page so that the mythical people that are using my can brag to each other about their followers. So, I needed to create some methods. Method #1 was pretty straightforward - I wanted to display how many followers each user has on their homepage.

For this I just needed to create a simple method in the User model:

def total_followers
    self.followers.length
end

Here I'm just taking current user and accessing all of the followers they have through the has_many relationship and then grabbing the total number of those followers using .length. I then add total_followers to the user_serializer attributes, and display user.total_followers on the front and I have a super straightforward way to satisfy the narcissists on the app.

I created an equally simple approach to total up the number of people a user is following with method #2:

def total_users_you_follow
    self.users_they_follow.length
end

Now, it's not enough for people to just know how many people are following them - they also want to know who is following them. So, for this I went back to my user model and created method #3: 

def list_followers
    self.followers.map do |follower|
        follower.username
    end
end

In this instance, I'm taking the current user and I map through all of the followers and return an array of all of the usernames of those followers. Now, later on, I might want to pass on more than the names and perhaps set up a new serializer so that I can actually send along more follower data to display, but since this project has to be turned in in two days, I'm just gonna handle the names for now. I posted the names as a string by posting the following to the page user.list_followers.toString().

Again, I just had to slightly alter things to gather the same info for who the users are following and method #4:

def list_users_you_follow
    self.users_they_follow.map do |followed|
        followed.username
    end
end

OK, that was all working, but I still had one more issue I wanted to resolve. Each user page has a "Follow me" button that when clicked sends the info to the backend and enters the user_id for both the follower and who they're following in the follows table, but, at the moment there is no indication on the front end that anything has happened. So I want to set up a ternary to display "Follow me" if the current user doesn't follow the person whose page they're viewing and "You Follow" if they do not follow that person. So... how do I implement this?

My first thought was another method since I was on a roll with those, but then I realized I could further utilize a couple of the methods I'd just written.

In the UserPage.js component, I declared a new boolean variable:

let alreadyFollow = false

In the component, I already have a variable called userPageInfo that contains all of the into for the user whose page it is. So I can use the list_followers method to grab all of the followers for that user and then search through them:

 userPageInfo.list_followers.forEach((fol) => {

I could then just compare each follower username to the username of who is currently logged in

        if (fol === user.username)

and then if I find a match I simply change the already follow variable to true. Seems easy enough. Let's check to see if it works.

        TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined 
        (reading 'forEach')

Come on!! Why didn't that work? Oh... I just realized that the Userpage that I'm currently on is a user without any followers yet, so the forEach is trying to map through nothing and hitting the error. I can fix this!... I hope.

I just have to wrap the forEach loop in another if statement and I will once again call on one of the methods I've already written.

 if (userPageInfo.total_followers > 0)

So now, the forEach will only run if the User has followers and if not, the alreadyFollow boolean will stay false as desired. Let's check it out...

Success!! So here's final version of the if statement, forEach loop.

if (userPageInfo.total_followers > 0) {
    userPageInfo.list_followers.forEach((fol) => {
        if (fol === user.username) {
            alreadyFollow = true
            console.log(alreadyFollow)
        }
    })
}

And here is the ternary I use to put the correct follow button on the page:

<h5>
  {alreadyFollow ? <button> You Follow 
  {userPageInfo.username} </button>
  : <button onClick={handleFollow}>Follow 
  {userPageInfo.username}</button>}
</h5>

Perfect. So now everybody knows who's following who and I'll leave it to them to figure out why.

python is an open-source language, meaning its existing and new developments are freely available to everyone. Anyone can write some code and then make it available for others to download, install, and use. You might find these programs referred to as Python modules or Python packages. But what is the difference between these two terms? We’ll explore this question in this article.Modules and packages both make code reusable among many people. You can write a module to calculate the area of various shapes. And you can give this module to your friends so they can use it without having to rewrite all the code. This saves time and effort and helps avoid mistakes.
Learning Python was extremely difficult for me, but it didn’t have to be.

A little over a decade ago, I was a college graduate with a history degree and few prospects. Then, I became a successful machine learning engineer, data science consultant, and now CEO of Dataquest.This is not an overnight success story, though. My journey to learn Python was long, inefficient, and frequently discouraging.If I could do it over again, I would follow the steps I’m going to share with you in this article. It would have fast-tracked my career, saved thousands of hours of wasted time, and prevented a lot of stress.
Many of the courses out there make learning Python more difficult than it has to be. To illustrate my point, I’ll give you a personal example.

When I first started learning Python, I wanted to do the things that excited me, like making websites. Unfortunately, the course I was taking forced me to spend multiple months on syntax. It was agony.

Throughout the course, Python code continued to look foreign and confusing. It was like an alien language. It’s no surprise I quickly lost interest.

Regrettably, most Python tutorials are very similar to this. They assume you need to learn all of Python syntax before you can start doing anything interesting. Is it any wonder most people give up?

Instead of wasting time on these mundane tasks, you could be experiencing the real thrills of Python. Think analyzing data, building a website, or creating an autonomous drone with artificial intelligence!

错误 1 ： Cannot find symbol reactRootView.setIsFabric(BuildConfig.IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED;

解决方案：

  • 将“IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED”变量添加到 android> buildTypes > debug 和 android> buildTypes > release in app/build.gradle

备注：我们将此变量设置为 false 以禁用 react native 的新架构，如果你想使用它替换为 true。

解决方案：

  • 从 android/app 中删除构建文件夹

错误 3： INSTALL_FAILED_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE

解决方案：

  • 打开 AndroidStudio 并转到 SDK Manager

关闭模拟器并擦除手机数据

Yassine Chraa

Posted on Jan 23

The React Native Android build encountered multiple errors, leading to a failed build. These errors included missing dependencies, issues with the Android SDK configuration, and other errors that made the React Native development process challenging.

In this article, we present some similar errors that can appear in the Android build for React Native development and provide solutions to resolve them.

Error1: Cannot find symbol reactRootView.setIsFabric(BuildConfig.IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED;

Solution:

  • Add "IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED" variable to android> buildTypes > debug and android> buildTypes > release in app/build.gradle

Remark: we set this variable as false to disable the new architecture of react native if you want to use it replace with true.

Error2: Requested internal only, but not enough space;

Solution:

  • Remove build folder from android/app

Error3: INSTALL_FAILED_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE

Solution:

  • Open AndroidStudio and go to SDK Manager
